tasks_completed.description: Item C — verify spec-continuation marker parity across producer, consumer-prose, consumer-script. No gaps found; verify-only no-op.
tasks_completed.rationale: BA spec declared Item C a verify-only task with expected no-op. All three sites confirmed to use '<!-- spec-continuation-of: <task-id> -->' as the sole content predicate. No surviving literal task-id grep fallback found.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A - verify-only parity confirmation, no regression
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Step-7 predicate was previously migrated to the machine-readable marker; Item C confirms the migration is complete at all three sites.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: No fix needed — parity already in place. Confirmation prevents future drift assumptions.

evidence: Producer: commands/spec-update.md:54-61 — instructs writing '<!-- spec-continuation-of: <resolved-task-id> -->' as the very first line of the new cycle block, written exactly once per (task-id, spec file) pair. Sole continuation marker.

Consumer prose: commands/commit.md:222 — runs `grep -lF "<!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} -->" candidate.md` and explicitly states 'this is the ONLY content predicate allowed; no other grep, no free-form content scan.'
Consumer script: scripts/step7-spec-update.py:134 defines `marker = f"<!-- spec-continuation-of: {task_id} -->"`; :148 the only content predicate is `if marker in content`.

commit.md: the only `${TASK_ID}` grep in the stage-3 algorithm is the marker grep itself at :222 (grep -lF on the spec-continuation-of marker). No literal-task-id content grep or free-form content scan exists.

step7-spec-update.py: grep for `grep.*task_id`, `task_id in content`, `str(task_id)`, `f"{task_id}"` returned no matches. The bare task_id never appears as a content predicate — it is only interpolated into the marker string at :134.

dev.qa_notes: Verify-only Item C, no gaps found, zero files modified. QA can re-confirm parity at commands/spec-update.md:54-61 (producer), commands/commit.md:222 (consumer prose, sole content predicate), scripts/step7-spec-update.py:134-148 (consumer script, `if marker in content` sole predicate). AC-C1 and AC-C2 both pass with no surviving literal-task-id content grep.

self_verification.notes: All three cited sites confirmed at the exact BA file:line. Marker is the sole content predicate at producer (spec-update.md:54-61), consumer-prose (commit.md:222), consumer-script (step7-spec-update.py:134-148). No literal task-id grep fallback exists anywhere in the stage-3 algorithm. Zero files modified, consistent with the spec's expected no-op outcome.
